title: penstemon saltarius
intro: Penstemon saltarius, also known as Salt Spring beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is often used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating respiratory issues, and can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures. Herbal preparations of Penstemon saltarius are typically made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which are harvested during the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
